queries:
SELECT COALESCE(p.column_name, u.column_name) AS column_name,
                   p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position, p.data_type, u.data_type
            FROM (SELECT * FROM PROD_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'GENERAL_JOB_ROLE') p
            FULL JOIN (SELECT * FROM UAT_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'GENERAL_JOB_ROLE') u
              ON p.column_name = u.column_name
            WHERE p.column_name IS NULL OR u.column_name IS NULL
               OR p.ordinal_position != u.ordinal_position
               OR p.data_type != u.data_type
            ORDER BY COALESCE(p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position)

SELECT p.n, u.n, p.n - u.n
            FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) p,
                 (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) u

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", COUNT(*) AS n FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE
                GROUP BY ALL HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
                ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", COUNT(*) AS n FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE
                GROUP BY ALL HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
                ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100

T3.cte:
WITH prod AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region),
     uat AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region)

accepted_reasons.Job Role Modified Date: Affected by MAYDAY-526 - Missing April 29th - UAT is correct

T4.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))) AS "Job Role Title",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))) AS "Job Role Description",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))) AS "Job Role Identifier",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))) AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Created Date", u."Job Role Created Date")) AS "Job Role Created Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")) AS "Job Role Modified Date"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")

Job Role Description: mornington
SYD

The Fleet Co-ordinator at Mornington Shire Council plays a pivotal role in managing and optimising the council's fleet of vehicles and equipment. This position requires an individual who is highly organised and possesses excellent communication skills, as they will liaise with various departments to ensure that all operational requirements are met efficiently.Key responsibilities include:Overseeing the maintenance, repair, and servicing of the council’s fleet to ensure reliability and safety.Coordinating vehicle procurement, disposal, and inventory management to maintain an efficient fleet.Developing and implementing fleet policies and procedures in line with council regulations.Monitoring fuel consumption and managing associated records to promote operational efficiency.Conducting regular audits of vehicles and equipment for compliance with safety standards.Maintaining accurate records of vehicle usage, maintenance schedules, and costs involved.Collaborating with external service providers for repairs, inspections, and parts supply as needed.Providing training and support to staff on the appropriate use of vehicles and equipment.This role is essential in ensuring that the Mornington Shire Council operates a safe, effective, and sustainable fleet that meets the needs of its community services while adhering to budgetary constraints.

The Fleet Co-ordinator at Mornington Shire Council plays a pivotal role in managing and optimising the council's fleet of vehicles and equipment. This position requires an individual who is highly organised and possesses excellent communication skills, as they will liaise with various departments to ensure that all operational requirements are met efficiently. Key responsibilities include: Overseeing the maintenance, repair, and servicing of the council’s fleet to ensure reliability and safety. Coordinating vehicle procurement, disposal, and inventory management to maintain an efficient fleet. Developing and implementing fleet policies and procedures in line with council regulations. Monitoring fuel consumption and managing associated records to promote operational efficiency. Conducting regular audits of vehicles and equipment for compliance with safety standards. Maintaining accurate records of vehicle usage, maintenance schedules, and costs involved. Collaborating with external service providers for repairs, inspections, and parts supply as needed. Providing training and support to staff on the appropriate use of vehicles and equipment. This role is essential in ensuring that the Mornington Shire Council operates a safe, effective, and sustainable fleet that meets the needs of its community services while adhering to budgetary constraints.

Executive Manager Practice, Quality & Services will provide strategic and operational leadership to, and oversee the management of, a suite of programs within LC ensuring the quality of services delivered are of the highest standard. This includes complying with current legislation, accreditation and contractual requirements. With particular emphasis on strategic direction and future planning, this position sits within the Executive Team and will actively participate in and contribute to the broader organisational Management team.

mornington
SYD

Position Description:The Manager Infrastructure Delivery at Mornington Shire Council is a pivotal role responsible for overseeing the planning, implementation, and management of infrastructure projects within the council's jurisdiction. This position requires strategic oversight and effective leadership to ensure that all infrastructure initiatives align with the council's objectives and serve the community's needs.Main Responsibilities:Lead and manage a team of professionals in the delivery of infrastructure projects, ensuring high standards of quality, safety, and efficiency.Develop and implement project plans from conception through to completion, including budgeting, scheduling, and resource allocation.Coordinate with various stakeholders including internal departments, contractors, government agencies, and community groups to facilitate smooth project execution.Monitor project progress against timelines and budgets; provide regular updates to senior management on status and any potential issues.Ensure compliance with relevant regulations, policies, and standards throughout all phases of project delivery.Identify risks associated with infrastructure projects and develop mitigation strategies to address them effectively.Foster a culture of continuous improvement within the team by implementing best practices in project management methodologies.This role requires strong leadership skills combined with extensive knowledge of infrastructure development processes. The ideal candidate will demonstrate an ability to motivate teams while maintaining productive relationships with stakeholders across various sectors. The Manager Infrastructure Delivery plays a crucial part in shaping sustainable infrastructures that enhance the quality of life for residents within Mornington Shire Council.

Position Description: The Manager Infrastructure Delivery at Mornington Shire Council is a pivotal role responsible for overseeing the planning, implementation, and management of infrastructure projects within the council's jurisdiction. This position requires strategic oversight and effective leadership to ensure that all infrastructure initiatives align with the council's objectives and serve the community's needs. Main Responsibilities: Lead and manage a team of professionals in the delivery of infrastructure projects, ensuring high standards of quality, safety, and efficiency. Develop and implement project plans from conception through to completion, including budgeting, scheduling, and resource allocation. Coordinate with various stakeholders including internal departments, contractors, government agencies, and community groups to facilitate smooth project execution. Monitor project progress against timelines and budgets; provide regular updates to senior management on status and any potential issues. Ensure compliance with relevant regulations, policies, and standards throughout all phases of project delivery. Identify risks associated with infrastructure projects and develop mitigation strategies to address them effectively. Foster a culture of continuous improvement within the team by implementing best practices in project management methodologies. This role requires strong leadership skills combined with extensive knowledge of infrastructure development processes. The ideal candidate will demonstrate an ability to motivate teams while maintaining productive relationships with stakeholders across various sectors. The Manager Infrastructure Delivery plays a crucial part in shaping sustainable infrastructures that enhance the quality of life for residents within Mornington Shire Council.
lccare
SYD

The Executive Manager Community Services provides organisation-wide strategic and operational leadership across Lutheran Care’s community services portfolio in South Australia and the Northern Territory, with responsibility for all Government acquitted services that sit outside of the organisation’s commercial operations. The role is accountable for ensuring services are high quality, safe, sustainable and aligned to Lutheran Care’s strategic priorities. The role requires influential executive leadership. It sets direction, engages leaders and drives performance within a complex service environment. This includes leading performance across a multi-layered workforce and ensuring effective structures, leadership capability and workforce practices that support high quality service delivery and sustainable outcomes. As a member of the Executive Team, the role contributes to organisational strategy, risk management and decision making. It also represents Lutheran Care at senior levels across government, funding bodies and the sector.

gmeau
SYD

The Inwards Goods Quality Control Coordinator’s primary goal is to manage, execute, and safeguard the incoming material inspection process, ensuring that both Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines are continuously supplied with fully verified and compliant components. This role owns the raw material quality lifecycle at the point of receipt, coordinates defect containment and material sorting, maintains engineering "golden samples," and collaborates closely with the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer to process non-conformances and vendor replacements. Additionally, this position serves in a secondary capacity to provide critical operational coverage and support for the Production Quality Control Coordinator, ensuring seamless continuity across data entry, ESD control, and manufacturing documentation compliance.Job Responsibilities:1. Primary Focus: Inwards Goods Management & Material InspectionPriority & Flow Management: Reviews the Incoming Material List to identify, schedule, and execute inspection priorities, ensuring a reliable supply of materials to Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines. Order & Specification Verification: Reviews shipping details, order numbers, and quantities against engineering drawings, technical specifications, and purchase orders. AQL & Dimensional Inspections: Obtains reference drawings and samples to inspect incoming parts, executing critical dimension and finish checks using specialised measurement tools and instrumentation in strict adherence to AQL guidelines. Golden Sample Management: Manages and maintains the Manufacturing & Product Engineering (MPE) sample framework, ensuring "golden samples" are properly stored, controlled, and accessible as per company guidelines.Cross-functional Liaison: Liaises proactively with Engineering, Warehouse, and Production staff to facilitate efficient material inspection outcomes and minimise manufacturing downtime. 2. Primary Focus: Defect Containment, Sorting & NCR SupportIssue Containment & Sorting: Executes immediate inspection, isolation, and sorting of materials whenever a quality issue is identified or suspected on the floor or at receipt.Escalation & Communication: Communicates material defects, test failures, and variances directly to the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer for systemic evaluation. NCR & Replacement Coordination: Generates Rejected Material Advice notifications through the material management system and actively coordinates material replacements, supplier returns, and Non-Conformance Report (NCR) documentation. 3. Secondary Focus: Production Quality Coverage & SupportProduction Coordinator Coverage: Cross-trains to actively cover and support the Production Quality Control Coordinator during peak periods or absences. Quality Data Entry: Assists with standard quality data entry, capturing failure metrics and process logs to support graphical performance reporting. ESD Control Support: Assists with Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) compliance verifications and standard control measures across manufacturing zones.Production Documentation Verification: Participates in verifying production documentation compliance, reviewing manufacturing line logs and building records for completeness. 4. General & Regulatory ComplianceLegislative Compliance: Follows all Quality, Work Health & Safety (WHS), and Environment legislation, technical standards, and regulatory requirements. QA Evolution: Undertakes targeted tasks related to quality matters as directed by the department manager to support the continuous evolution of the company. Safety, Environment, Quality and Compliance ResponsibilitiesSafety Adherence: Ensures the safety of self, work colleagues, and the environment by following all workplace guidelines and safety criteria. Site Rules: Operates in complete accordance with company site rules, standard operating procedures, and established safety policies. Escalation: Promptly reports and escalates safety, environment, compliance, and quality concerns, incidents, or near misses.

The Inwards Goods Quality Control Coordinator’s primary goal is to manage, execute, and safeguard the incoming material inspection process, ensuring that both Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines are continuously supplied with fully verified and compliant components. This role owns the raw material quality lifecycle at the point of receipt, coordinates defect containment and material sorting, maintains engineering "golden samples," and collaborates closely with the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer to process non-conformances and vendor replacements. Additionally, this position serves in a secondary capacity to provide critical operational coverage and support for the Production Quality Control Coordinator, ensuring seamless continuity across data entry, ESD control, and manufacturing documentation compliance. Primary Focus: Inwards Goods Management & Material Inspection Priority & Flow Management: Reviews the Incoming Material List to identify, schedule, and execute inspection priorities, ensuring a reliable supply of materials to Commercial Product and Contract Manufacturing production lines. Order & Specification Verification: Reviews shipping details, order numbers, and quantities against engineering drawings, technical specifications, and purchase orders. Cross-functional Liaison: Liaises proactively with Engineering, Warehouse, and Production staff to facilitate efficient material inspection outcomes and minimise manufacturing downtime. 2. Primary Focus: Defect Containment, Sorting & NCR Support Issue Containment & Sorting: Executes immediate inspection, isolation, and sorting of materials whenever a quality issue is identified or suspected on the floor or at receipt. Escalation & Communication: Communicates material defects, test failures, and variances directly to the Senior Quality Assurance Engineer for systemic evaluation. NCR & Replacement Coordination: Generates Rejected Material Advice notifications through the material management system and actively coordinates material replacements, supplier returns, and Non-Conformance Report (NCR) documentation. 3. Secondary Focus: Production Quality Coverage & Support Production Coordinator Coverage: Cross-trains to actively cover and support the Production Quality Control Coordinator during peak periods or absences. Quality Data Entry: Assists with standard quality data entry, capturing failure metrics and process logs to support graphical performance reporting. ESD Control Support: Assists with Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) compliance verifications and standard control measures across manufacturing zones. Production Documentation Verification: Participates in verifying production documentation compliance, reviewing manufacturing line logs and building records for completeness. 4. General & Regulatory Compliance Legislative Compliance: Follows all Quality, Work Health & Safety (WHS), and Environment legislation, technical standards, and regulatory requirements. QA Evolution: Undertakes targeted tasks related to quality matters as directed by the department manager to support the continuous evolution of the company. Safety, Environment, Quality and Compliance Responsibilities Safety Adherence: Ensures the safety of self, work colleagues, and the environment by following all workplace guidelines and safety criteria. Site Rules: Operates in complete accordance with company site rules, standard operating procedures, and established safety policies. Escalation: Promptly reports and escalates safety, environment, compliance, and quality concerns, incidents, or near misses.
Job Role Title: Executive Manager Quality Risk & Intermediary Services

queries:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))) AS "Job Role Title",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))) AS "Job Role Description",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))) AS "Job Role Identifier",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))) AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Created Date", u."Job Role Created Date")) AS "Job Role Created Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")) AS "Job Role Modified Date"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Skills and Experience" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Skills and Experience" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
WHERE NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))
LIMIT 5

JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")

SELECT "Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION", p."Job Role Identifier" AS prod_value, u."Job Role Identifier" AS uat_value
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
